investigation.investigation_summary: SEEK Limited (ASX: SEK) closed at A$12.71 on 22 May 2026, down 5.85% for the session, with the stock opening at A$13.42 and falling to an intraday low of A$12.70 on volume of roughly 1.32 million shares *** (SEEK Investor Centre, 22 May 2026). No company-specific announcement, press release, or regulatory filing dated 22 May 2026 was identified that directly explains the decline. The most recent material corporate news remains the FY26 half-year result released on 17 February 2026, when SEEK reported a statutory net loss of A$249.9 million driven largely by a Zhaopin impairment, while adjusted profit rose to A$104.5 million and management upgraded full-year revenue guidance to A$1.19 billion–A$1.23 billion ** (RTT News, 17 Feb 2026). That result highlighted declining paid job-ad volumes in Australia & New Zealand and Asia, with revenue growth coming from yield expansion rather than volume *(Rask Media, 17 Feb 2026)*. The stock has been under sustained selling pressure, having fallen approximately 46% over the preceding 52 weeks as of 22 May 2026 *(stockanalysis.com, 22 May 2026)*. As of 13 May 2026, data provider stockanalysis.com listed the next estimated earnings date as 22 May 2026, but by 22 May 2026 the site had revised the estimate to 29 May 2026 *(stockanalysis.com, 13 May & 22 May 2026)*. A change in substantial holding was also announced on 7 May 2026 *(Intelligent Investor, 7 May 2026)*. Without a clear same-day catalyst, the move is most plausibly a continuation of negative sentiment around labour-market softness and the stock’s poor year-to-date trend, though this remains speculative.
established_facts: SEEK Limited (ASX: SEK) closed at A$12.71 on 22 May 2026, down 5.85% for the session, opening at A$13.42 and trading as low as A$12.70 with volume of 1,320,440 shares *** (SEEK Investor Centre, 22 May 2026).

SEEK reported FY26 half-year results on 17 February 2026, posting a statutory net loss of A$249.9 million due to a Zhaopin impairment, while adjusted profit rose to A$104.5 million and full-year revenue guidance was upgraded to A$1.19 billion–A$1.23 billion ** (RTT News, 17 Feb 2026).
The H1 FY26 result highlighted declining paid job-ad volumes in Australia & New Zealand and Asia, with revenue growth driven by yield expansion rather than volume *(Rask Media, 17 Feb 2026)*.
The stock had fallen approximately 46% over the 52 weeks preceding 22 May 2026 *(stockanalysis.com, 22 May 2026)*.
A change in substantial holding was announced on 7 May 2026 *(Intelligent Investor, 7 May 2026)*.

candidate_causes.cause: Continuation of negative sentiment around declining job-ad volumes and yield-driven revenue growth
candidate_causes.supporting_evidence: SEEK’s H1 FY26 results (17 Feb 2026) showed paid job-ad volumes fell in ANZ and Asia, and the stock has declined ~46% over the past year, suggesting ongoing investor concern about labour-market softness *(Rask Media, 17 Feb 2026; stockanalysis.com, 22 May 2026)*.

candidate_causes.cause: Positioning ahead of an anticipated earnings release that was initially estimated for 22 May 2026
candidate_causes.supporting_evidence: Data provider stockanalysis.com listed the next estimated earnings date as 22 May 2026 on 13 May 2026, but updated it to 29 May 2026 later on 22 May 2026 *(stockanalysis.com, 13 May & 22 May 2026)*.

investigation.relationship_to_existing_thesis: No Stage 3 investment thesis was provided for SEK.AX. The observed price move cannot be evaluated against a pre-existing thesis, and no clearly new, thesis-altering information was identified for the current session.
investigation.ambiguity_notes: No specific news item, SEC/ASX filing, or broker action dated 22 May 2026 was found to explain the ~6% intraday drop. The decline may reflect a continuation of the negative sentiment around declining job-ad volumes and the stock’s poor year-to-date performance, but a direct catalyst remains unverified.

decision.rationale:
Call 1 established no material facts dated 22 May 2026 that are supported by high-credibility sources. The only primary-source fact (***) records the price action itself—closing at A$12.71, down 5.85%, on volume of 1.32 million shares—which confirms the mechanical trigger but offers no explanatory causation. The remaining established facts are either stale (the 17 February 2026 H1 FY26 results reported by RTT News **) or derived from low-credibility sources (*), such as the 52-week decline context and a data-provider earnings-date estimate revision. None constitute new, thesis-altering information.

There is no existing Stage 3 investment thesis for SEK.AX, which means there are no embedded structural assumptions or anchor-price scenarios to challenge or invalidate. The candidate causes offered by Call 1—continuation of negative sentiment around declining job-ad volumes and positioning around an estimated earnings release—are speculative and explicitly assigned low to medium confidence. Both rely on secondary or data-aggregator sources (*) rather than primary company disclosures or established news outlets. Without an anchor price from a pre-existing thesis, the >15% cumulative-move threshold for a ‘thesis built at the wrong price’ cannot be invoked, and the single-session decline of 5.85% alone is insufficient to override the default posture.

Given the absence of new, high-credibility information and Call 1’s own low-confidence assessment—coupled with ambiguity notes stating that a direct catalyst remains unverified—rerunning the pipeline would risk an expensive false positive driven purely by price momentum. The decision criteria require clear evidence of a material new fact or a challenged structural assumption before leaning toward rerun; neither condition is met.
key_facts_relied_on: SEEK Limited (ASX: SEK) closed at A$12.71 on 22 May 2026, down 5.85% for the session, opening at A$13.42 and trading as low as A$12.70 with volume of 1,320,440 shares *** (SEEK Investor Centre, 22 May 2026)

SEEK reported FY26 half-year results on 17 February 2026, posting a statutory net loss of A$249.9 million due to a Zhaopin impairment, while adjusted profit rose to A$104.5 million and full-year revenue guidance was upgraded to A$1.19 billion–A$1.23 billion ** (RTT News, 17 Feb 2026)
The H1 FY26 result highlighted declining paid job-ad volumes in Australia & New Zealand and Asia, with revenue growth driven by yield expansion rather than volume *(Rask Media, 17 Feb 2026)*
stockanalysis.com listed the next estimated earnings date as 22 May 2026 on 13 May 2026, but updated it to 29 May 2026 on 22 May 2026 *(stockanalysis.com, 13 May & 22 May 2026)*
decision.uncertainty_acknowledged: The stock has fallen ~46% over the preceding 52 weeks and declined ~6% on elevated volume during the session, which could hint at an unreported emerging issue or informed selling. However, Call 1 could not verify any catalyst and assigned low confidence to its investigation. In the absence of high-credibility sourcing, acting on price action alone would be speculative.
decision.considered_alternative: The alternative case is that a sharp single-day drop on high volume with no obvious news sometimes signals an impending negative event—such as an earnings warning—and that the cumulative drift from any reasonable entry price likely exceeds 15%, warranting a fresh valuation pass. But the earnings-date shift comes from a low-credibility data provider, not the company, and the negative sentiment driver is four months old. Rerunning on this basis would set a low bar that invites false positives.
